name: Bouillon Chartier Grands Boulevards
summary: A no-reservation Paris classic for inexpensive traditional French food in a historic Belle Epoque room.
whyItMatters: For the Paris portion, this is a practical fallback that still feels memorable: open daily, continuous service, modest prices, and no booking dependency.
pros: No reservations and continuous service make it flexible
Good value for a Paris group meal
cons: No reservations means lines are part of the experience
Shared tables and quick service may not fit a leisurely final dinner

logistics.reservation_needed: No; official site says service continu sans reservation.
logistics.distance_from_base: Unknown until Paris lodging is confirmed.
logistics.distance_from_viewing_point: Not a race-day viewing food option.
logistics.transit: Official site lists Metro Grands Boulevards lines 8/9 and buses 74/48.
logistics.road_closure_notes: No Tour de France closure relevance found for this non-viewing candidate.

timing.best_visit_window: Paris lunch or dinner when flexibility matters more than reservations.
timing.race_day_risk: Low unless it conflicts with Paris arrival/departure timing.

sources.title: Bouillon Chartier official website
sources.note: Supports concept, hours, no-reservation policy, addresses, transit notes, and value positioning.

sources.title: Bouillon Chartier Wikipedia
sources.note: Supports historical context including 1896 founding and historic restaurant background; secondary source only.

open_questions: Confirm whether the Grands Boulevards branch is closest to the final Paris hotel; Montparnasse or Gare de l'Est may be more practical depending on lodging.
